title: tpm — faaas_kiwi_project_pptx — v1
description: Synthesized by skill_builder for persona=tpm, kb=faaas_kiwi_project_pptx. Refine before promoting.

reporting_date.description: Extract the most explicit 'as of' / last-updated date from the Confluence page; if none is stated, use the most recent dated event mentioned on the page (e.g., review/meeting date) and label it as 'As of <date> (inferred from latest dated update)'.

project_name.description: Extract the program/project name exactly as written in the Confluence page title or header.

project_purpose.description: Extract the stated purpose/goal of the project in one exec-safe sentence; expand acronyms on first use if present in the source text.

rag_summary.description: Derive a single overall RAG (Red/Amber/Green) for the program by summarising the Confluence workstreams (#1 security scanning, #2 fleet patching, #3 DB patching, #4 OS patching). Always include the RAG label plus exactly one sentence justification grounded in: completion metrics (e.g., scan % and counts), explicit capacity gaps (e.g., slots available vs needed), and any leadership decisions/dependencies called out.

schedule_health.description: Derive schedule health in 1–2 exec-safe sentences by combining: (a) security scanning completion (% complete and remaining repos), (b) any dated checkpoints (e.g., review date, working group meeting date), and (c) any stated constraints that could delay completion (e.g., patching capacity). Avoid passive voice.

orm_status.description: If the Confluence page explicitly states an ORM status/label, extract it verbatim; otherwise return 'Not stated in source'. Do not infer ORM status.

status_highlights.description: Derive 3–5 concise exec-facing status bullets by summarising the four workstreams on the Confluence page. Each bullet must include a concrete metric or named deliverable when available (e.g., '89% complete; 237/266 repos scanned').

workstreams.description: Extract the list of workstreams from the Confluence page and summarise each in exec-safe language. For each workstream, include: name/number, current status (with key metric if present), and the most important dependency/decision if stated. Derive this by reading the four workstream sections (#1–#4) and condensing each to 2–3 lines.

blocking_issues.description: Derive blockers by aggregating items in the Confluence page that indicate 'need clarity/decision', 'pending', 'only X slots available', 'ERs needed', or other explicit dependencies. For each blocker, include: description, owner (team or named individual if stated), target_resolution (date if stated; else 'TBD'), and critical_path (true if it gates scanning/patching completion; else false). If none, return an empty array.

next_steps.description: Derive 3–6 time-bound next steps by combining action-oriented statements across the Confluence workstreams (e.g., file ERs, schedule/hold working group, complete remaining scans, decide patching cadence). Each item must include an explicit timeframe ('by <date>' if present, otherwise 'this sprint' or 'next 1–2 weeks') and an owner if stated; do not fabricate dates.

exec_asks.description: Derive executive asks by aggregating explicit leadership asks/decisions in the Confluence page (e.g., monthly full fleet patching proposal decision, clarity on DB patching requirement and patching types). Each ask must be a single sentence starting with an action verb and include why it matters in a short clause.

key_metrics.description: Extract the most exec-relevant quantitative metrics from the Confluence page (scan completion %, repos scanned/total/remaining; DB ksplice completion for prod/non-prod/demo; patching capacity slots available vs needed). Represent each metric as a short label plus value; do not add metrics not present in the source.

key_links.description: Extract operational links from the Confluence page (PMO ticket, Jira dashboard/filter, security dashboard, repo list, Slack channels, DevOps code repository, supporting XLS, attachment image link). Provide as a list of {label, url} derived from the page content.
